name: Sierra Sawyer
ai_summary: Sierra brings 9 years of childcare experience, primarily with foster children and those with behavioral issues, and is located in Tucson, AZ. She uses positive reinforcement and ABA-informed techniques to understand and redirect behavior, making her a strong fit for the role.

transcripts.q3:
My name is Sierra. I have worked in the childcare industry for nine years. I work with foster kids. I worked with kids with disabilities. I've also worked the kids with behavioral issues. I've worked with all ages. I really enjoyed working with kids because I feel like I can relate to kids more than I can with adults. And I like to put myself in their shoes. I'm very outgoing.
 A very bubbly person and I'm still pretty young. So I feel like the kids can come to me about anything.
transcripts.q4: I am located in Tucson Arizona, Central East Side, Fifth and Swan area.
transcripts.q5: Yes, I am open to 4 or part-time any shifts any days. I am available.
transcripts.q6: Yes, I have. And for that scenario, I would say if one kid, get another kid, I would tell them, hey, we can't do that. We have to keep our hands to ourselves. We don't hit our friends. If we have any issues, we talk about it with the teacher or we bring the teacher and we have a conversation and we sit down and I'll talk
transcripts.q7:
like I was saying before with my experience working with kids and working with kids that have behavioral issues and being able to understand them what makes me a great teacher is
 I like to redirect, I like to use positive reinforcement. I've worked in ABA therapy. So I
 Like to understand how a child thinks and why they think that way? And what made them? What triggered them to act this way? So that's what myself in the kids shoes. I like to conversate and listen to them instead of reacting first and not listening.
